summary: An article examining the health impacts of low-carbohydrate diets. Dr. Frank B. Hu cautions against traditional low-carb diets that rely heavily on red meat, advocating instead for a balance of whole grains, healthy fats, and plant-based proteins.

principles.statement: A healthy low-carbohydrate diet should emphasize whole grains, healthy sources of fat, and plant-based proteins.
principles.rationale: This macronutrient composition helps achieve weight loss and slows long-term weight gain without the cardiovascular and diabetes risks associated with high saturated fat intake.

signature_quotes.text: Traditional low-carb diets are loaded with red meat,
signature_quotes.context: Discussing how restricting carbohydrates often causes people to replace them with less healthy foods high in saturated fats.

anti_patterns.description: Following traditional low-carb diets that replace carbohydrates with red meat.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Consuming too much saturated fat from red meat can increase the risk of developing Type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease.
